pokemon:aipom.name: Aipom
pokemon:aipom.summary: Aipom’s tail ends in a hand-like appendage that can be cleverly manipulated. However, because the Pokémon uses its tail so much, its real hands have become rather clumsy.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Aipom’s tail ends in a hand-like appendage that can be cleverly manipulated. However, because the Pokémon uses its tail so much, its real hands have become rather clumsy.
It lives high among the treetops. It can use its tail as freely and cleverly as its hands.
It lives high among the treetops. It can use its tail as freely and cleverly as its hands.
It uses its tail to hang on to tree branches. It uses its momentum to swing from one branch to another.
It uses its tail to pluck fruits that are out of reach. Its tail is more adept than its real hands.
Its tail ends with a dexterous, handlike appendage. However, because it uses the tail so much, AIPOM’s real hands have become rather clumsy.
It lives atop tall trees. When leaping from branch to branch, it deftly uses its tail for balance.
Its tail is so powerful that it can use it to grab a tree branch and hold itself up in the air.
Its tail is so powerful that it can use it to grab a tree branch and hold itself up in the air.
Its tail is so powerful that it can use it to grab a tree branch and hold itself up in the air.
This treetop dweller possesses a tail as dexterous as a hand. Ancient writings describe this Pokémon as a one-armed oddity.
Aipom’s tail ends in a hand-like appendage that can be cleverly manipulated. However, because the Pokémon uses its tail so much, its real hands have become rather clumsy.
It lives atop giant trees. It wraps its tail around a branch so it won’t fall off while asleep.
It lives high among the treetops. It can use its tail as freely and cleverly as its hands.
AIPOM’s tail ends in a hand-like appendage that can be cleverly manipulated. However, because the POKéMON uses its tail so much, its real hands have become rather clumsy.
AIPOM’s tail ends in a hand-like appendage that can be cleverly manipulated. However, because the POKéMON uses its tail so much, its real hands have become rather clumsy.
It lives atop tall trees. When leap­ ing from branch to branch, it deftly uses its tail for balance.
It lives atop tall trees. When leaping from branch to branch, it deftly uses its tail for balance.
It searches for prey from the tops of trees. When it spots its favorite food, Bounsweet, Aipom gets excited and pounces.
As it did more and more with its tail, its hands became clumsy. It makes its nest high in the treetops.
It lives high among the treetops. It can use its tail as freely and cleverly as its hands.
It lives high among the treetops. It can use its tail as freely and cleverly as its hands.
It lives atop tall trees. When leaping from branch to branch, it deftly uses its tail for balance.
Its tail is so powerful that it can use it to grab a tree branch and hold itself up in the air.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Aipom’s tail ends in a hand-like appendage that can be cleverly manipulated. However, because the Pokémon uses its tail so much, its real hands have become rather clumsy.

pokemon:ambipom.name: Ambipom
pokemon:ambipom.summary: To eat, it deftly shucks nuts with its two tails. It rarely uses its arms now.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: To eat, it deftly shucks nuts with its two tails. It rarely uses its arms now.
Split into two, the tails are so adept at handling and doing things, Ambipom rarely uses its hands.
Split into two, the tails are so adept at handling and doing things, Ambipom rarely uses its hands.
To eat, it deftly shucks nuts with its two tails. It rarely uses its arms now.
They live on large trees. They are said to communicate by connecting their tails to those of others.
To affirm their kinship, members of this species will form a ring by linking their newly doubled tails together. On rare occasions, humans have been accepted into such rings.
They work in large colonies and make rings by linking their tails, apparently in friendship.
They work in large colonies and make rings by linking their tails, apparently in friendship.
Split into two, the tails are so adept at handling and doing things, AMBIPOM rarely uses its hands.
It is very difficult to dodge the consecutive strikes of its two tails.
It uses its tails for everything. If it wraps both of its tails around you and gives you a squeeze, that’s proof it really likes you.
In their search for comfortable trees, they get into territorial disputes with groups of Passimian. They win about half the time.
Split into two, the tails are so adept at handling and doing things, Ambipom rarely uses its hands.
Split into two, the tails are so adept at handling and doing things, Ambipom rarely uses its hands.
They work in large colonies and make rings by linking their tails, apparently in friendship.
To eat, it deftly shucks nuts with its two tails. It rarely uses its arms now.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: To eat, it deftly shucks nuts with its two tails. It rarely uses its arms now.
